name: Todd Russell
title: Mechanical Engineer
tagline: Multidisciplinary mechanical, mechatronics, and process engineer solving complex problems across automation, machine vision, and precision manufacturing, from R&D through production.

highlights: Started as an intern and was promoted to Engineer II, then Engineer III, within one year.

Drove a 35% relative improvement in initial module efficiency and a 35× improvement in module degradation lifespan as lead of module development, owning all loss mechanisms related to modulization of the solar cell.
Achieved a world-record 24.0% efficiency perovskite module through joint development with NREL.
Increased laser scribing throughput by 500% and yield by 5% and improved 6σ positional repeatability from 60μm to 20μm through automation and machine vision integration.
Spearheaded a company-wide defect tracking initiative, identifying two defect types present on 50% of modules driving 20× the degradation impact of all other categories combined.
Developed a user-friendly prototype to collect patient data and evaluate a non-invasive method of measuring intracranial pressure via ultrasound in patients with acute neurologic injury.
Conducted end-user interviews comparing existing solutions to inform design decisions.
Designed and modeled bespoke soundproofing, heat recovery, and controlled climate enclosure systems in SolidWorks for corrugated cardboard production lines.
Built master assemblies of 1,000+ individual components nested within subassemblies and improved the company's pack-and-go subassembly workflow.

experience.company: National Security Innovation Network, U.S. Air Force
highlights: Lead author of a cost-benefit analysis on stationing aerospace ground equipment at Gila Bend Auxiliary Airfield to prepare diverted F-35 aircraft for return to Luke Air Force Base.
Recommended strategy yielded a net annual saving of $20,692.68 (73%) and was implemented at Luke AFB with planned nationwide rollout.

experience.company: Greenaction for Health and Environmental Justice
highlights: Co-authored a report detailing how climate change processes including sea-level rise and groundwater rise will reactivate historically contaminated sites across the San Francisco Bay Area.

Primary researcher and author of the Bayview Hunters Point case study, covering decades of radiological and industrial contamination from the Hunters Point Naval Shipyard, the Tetra Tech data-falsification scandal, and ongoing environmental injustice.
Report adopted by Greenaction to advocate for government and press action on contamination cleanup before climate change worsens its effects on vulnerable communities.
Developed a black-powderless recovery system for the team's 9 km rocket, designing a motor-driven lead-screw mechanism to puncture a CO2 canister for parachute deployment.
Modeled descent characteristics under varying parachute parameters using MATLAB to size the drogue and main chutes.

experience.company: Mechanical Engineering Competition Team at Berkeley
highlights: Designed, proposed, and manufactured an EVA tool attachment mechanism for astronaut utility belts for NASA's Micro-g NExT Challenge.
Editor-in-Chief of the technical report; selected for Phase 2 and tested at NASA's Neutral Buoyancy Laboratory.

education.degree: M.S. Mechanical Engineering · Certificate of Engineering Leadership
education.details: Gordon Fellow, Gordon Institute of Engineering Leadership
GPA 3.89 / 4.0 · Cum Laude (Top 10%) · Dean's List, College of Engineering

coursework: Living Materials and their Biomaterial Replacements

publications.title: C60-based ionic salt electron shuttle for high-performance inverted perovskite solar modules
